entry.title: Bank Statement Date in Closed Period
entry.content:
Hi Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue related to the processing of bank statements and would appreciate your support in resolving it.

The uploaded bank statement has a statement date of 11.12.2025. When attempting to reprocess the bank statement items, the system generates an error message indicating that the posting period is closed.

As reopening an already closed period is not a preferred option, we would like to process and post the bank statement using the currently open posting period, which is May 2026.

Could you please advise on how we can avoid these error messages and proceed with posting the bank statement in the current open period?

entry.title: Why ERP still defines the limits of AI in the enterprise
entry.content:
Introduction

There’s been a lot of noise around AI and how much it’s changing enterprise systems. It is undeniable that changes are here to stay. We’ve seen teams getting better forecasts, automating things that used to take forever, and catching issues earlier than before. Even we as an enterprise have evolved into something that I have no idea how it would function if AI suddenly disappeared one day.

But after a while, the conversation shifts. You can do countless of things with AI, and I think that is what everyone else is focusing on. And what else? What comes after checking whether AI works or not? What do you actually do once it's there? Getting recommendations is not the hard part anymore. You can have a model suggesting changes, flagging risks, or pointing at opportunities, and most of the time it makes sense. The problem shows up later, when you try to use that in real processes. That’s usually where things start to slow down, and where ERP suddenly matters again.

The Gap Between AI Insights and Execution

We’ve seen this happen more than once. Companies expect faster decisions because they have better insights, but once those decisions need to move through finance, operations, or logistics, everything gets more complicated. Systems don’t always adapt quickly, processes depend on each other, and what looked like a simple adjustment ends up taking way more effort than expected.

In many scenarios, the real challenge emerges when you have to close the execution gap, and not when you have to add more intelligence, as many people may think. It’s something that comes up all the time in conversations with our clients: the need is there, the intent is clear, but the underlying ERP landscape just isn’t ready to move at the same speed. Closing that gap between expectation and reality is where most of the real work is happening today.

The struggle that most organizations have has todo with what comes after they have produce insights. How do you execute a decision through a system that is so structured and interconnected? Is thanks to SAP S/4HANA —and similar ERP platforms— that decision has the opportunity to become reality.

On paper, the issue is simple. AI generates an insight, the business evaluates it, and then we, as organization, execute it. The thing is that, in reality, this chain encounters a vast amount of problems that lead to decisions getting delayed or partially implemented.

In one of the projects we worked on, this became very clear. The company, a global manufacturer in the energy space, was still relying on manual processes to count and track critical components. It wasn’t just slow, it also introduced errors and even safety concerns for the people involved.

By introducing AI-based image recognition, they were able to automate this process and significantly improve both productivity and safety. However, what became clear during the implementation is that the real value didn’t come from the AI model itself, but from how that information was integrated and executed across the operation. The data captured in the field needed to be synchronized with ERP systems, trigger updates in inventory, and align with warehouse and logistics processes in real time. Without that connection, the insight would have remained isolated. It was the ability to translate that intelligence into structured actions within the system that ultimately made the difference.

In another case, a client was using predictive models to anticipate demand changes and adjust procurement accordingly. The insights were accurate, but execution lagged behind. Translating those recommendations into actual purchase orders required multiple validations and manual interventions within the ERP system. By the time the adjustments were fully implemented, the conditions had already shifted. Once again, the limitation was not the quality of the insight, but the ability to execute it in time.

This is why the conversation around ERP is becoming relevant again, especially in the context of AI. It’s no longer just about having a system to run transactions, but about having a system that can absorb change and execute decisions without friction. Concepts like clean core, standardized processes, and flexible extensions are not just technical preferences anymore. They directly impact how fast and how effectively a company can act on what AI is telling them.

Conclusion

What we are seeing, then, is a shift in how these technologies relate to each other. AI is expanding what companies can see and understand about their business, giving them more options and better insights. ERP, on the other hand, continues to define what they can actually do with that information. The gap between those two layers is where many organizations are currently stuck.

The truth is that the companies that will get the most value out of AI are the ones that connect with the models to execution, and not necessarily the ones with the most advanced models. That means treating ERP not as a static backend system, but as a dynamic platform that enables change across the organization. AI might be redefining how companies think, but ERP still defines how they act. In the enterprise, action is where value is created.

entry.title: Re: Update Loading weight in physical stock to align with...
entry.content:
Hi,

Material master changes do not trigger any update to existing stock lines.
Hence, SAP advises such customizing should not be changed while products exist in the warehouse.

In this scenario HU/stock weight will only be updated when you move it.

Best Regards,
João

entry.title: Check out the Expert Guided Implementation (EGI) sessions for June 2026
entry.content:
Check out the Expert Guided Implementation (EGI’s) sessions for June 2026.  EGI’s are instructor led sessions.  The sessions below are designed to facilitate effective adoption and utilization of the SAP Signavio solutions.  EGIs are part of your SAP Enterprise Support or SAP Enterprise Support, Cloud Edition contract, which means there’s no additional cost for participating in these sessions.

Important:  The links in this blog connect to the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, which requires a one-time registration. 

📌  Sign up | Walkthrough video 

June 2026 sessions are outlined in this blog with brief descriptions. 

                                                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Plug and Gain approach – process discovery and analysis [English].  Register Here.  This offering provides participants with a practical, streamlines methodology for understanding and analyzing SAP ERP processes, leveraging SAP Signavio Process Insights, Process Intelligence and the Collaboration Hub.

Goals:

  • Discover and articulate the current as-is state of your business processes
  • Interpret key insights from the existing process landscape to support informed transformation decisions

                                                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Value Accelerators and Process Navigator [English].  Register Here.  This course helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Explorer to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. Also get an insight on country- and deployment-specific SAP solution value accelerators using Process Navigator, different deployment options, countries, and localization requirements.

Goals:  

  • Get SAP Signavio Process Explorer is set up and ready to use.
  • Understand how Value Accelerators can speed up your transformation journey.
  • Understand SAP Signavio Process Navigator.
  • Understand how to Navigate through different value accelerators​​.

                                                   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️ SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Sales [Portuguese and Spanish].  Register Here.  This offering helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Insights to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. Learn How to analyze your Lead-to-Cash process with SAP Signavio Process Insights.

Goals: 

  • Analyze Lead-to-Cash process data for better decision-making.
  • Implement recommendations to optimize sales workflows.
  • Prepare for S/4HANA migration with data-driven insights.

                                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Manager and Collaboration Hub [Spanish, and Portuguese]. Register Here.  This course is designed to facilitate the effective adoption and utilization of SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub for process management and modeling.

 Goals: 

  • Learn about SAP Signavio and how you can initiate your organizations transformation journey with SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub.
  • Gain an understanding of SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub, including its usage, navigation, and setting up initial configurations.
  • Acquire the skills to create process models in SAP Signavio Process Manager using BPMN 2.0.
  • Familiarize yourself with other crucial SAP Signavio Solutions integral to your transformation efforts.

                                       ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Plant Maintenance [English].  Register Here.  Learn how to analyze your Acquire to Decommission process with SAP Signavio Process Insights.

Goals:  

  • Access and interpret Acquire-to-Decommission process data.   
  • Apply recommendations to improve maintenance efficiency. 
  • Prepare for S/4HANA migration using actionable data insights.

                                            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Finance [English].  Register Here.  This offering helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Insights to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. This session focuses on processes in Finance. 

Goals:  

  • Understand key financial process data and its implications.   
  • Apply recommendations to strengthen finance operations.     
  • Use data insights to support a smooth S/4HANA migration.

                                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Procurement [English].  Register Here.  This offering helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Insights to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. Learn How to analyze your Source-to-Pay process with SAP Signavio Process Insights. 

Goals:  

  • Explore available Source-to-Pay data and its business relevance.

Learn How to analyze your Plan-to-Fulfill process with SAP Signavio Process Insights.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

entry.title: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Ledger TXT File – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editi
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We are experiencing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition during the e-Ledger generation process.

When we generate the e-Ledger file via the “Run Statutory Reports” application, the closing journal entries are not included in the generated TXT file.

We have already maintained the year-end closing document type as “ZK” in the related configuration areas. However, the closing entries are still missing in the output file.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Open the “Run Statutory Reports” application.
  2. Select the related reporting period (December 2025).
  3. Execute the e-Ledger generation process.
  4. Download the generated TXT file.
  5. Check the file content.

Actual result:
The closing journal entries are missing in the TXT output.

Expected result:
The closing journal entries should be included in the generated e-Ledger TXT file.

This is a productive system issue and critical for us, since the e-Ledger must be submitted to GİB within the legal deadline.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or found a solution for this scenario?

Thank you.

Melissa.

entry.title: Re: Call transaction CS02 ignores AEN (AENNR) field
entry.content:
In your case, the difference is not a bug but rather how SAP transactions consume parameter IDs. CA02 (routing change) does read the parameter ID AEN (change number) when you call it with CALL TRANSACTION ... AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN. CS02 (BOM change), however, does not pick up AEN from the parameter ID in the same way. It expects the change number to be passed explicitly on the initial screen or via the transaction variant. That is why your SET PARAMETER ID 'AEN' works for CA02 but appears ignored in CS02.

To fix this, use parameter ID ECN or a BDC call to pass the change number. That will clear the warning and open CS02 with the correct change number.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - You Want to Export to Excel? Not Today. 🚫📊
entry.content:
@JefB 
With that I mean the approach I described in Level 4

  • Remove Space Files permissions from consumer roles in Datasphere by creating a custom Role. This prevents users from actively browsing and selecting models in the SAC Excel Add In or the Data Analyzer in SAC, effectively disabling self-service Excel analysis — while still allowing them to consume SAC stories normally.

With that you can prevent Users to open the Analytic Models for certain spaces within the Data Analyzer. So that means you cannot give permissions for every single analytic model but for entire datasphere spaces via Scoped Roles which don't have the Space Files Permission as described in my screenshots 🙂

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content: Hi Shiva, SAP support advised me to copy all the XML files in the "<INST_DIR>\setup\packagestemp" folder to the "<INST_DIR>\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\packages" folder and restart the SIA. After the restart, all the XML files disappeared from the "packages" folder. This, combined with the fact that the system tests were all positive, suggests that the upgrade was completed successfully. We hope there are no future problems. Thank you very much.

entry.title: Using Claude and GPT-4o in SAP Generative AI Hub: A Complete Setup Guide
entry.content:
SAP COMMUNITY  |  SAP AI CORE  |  GENERATIVE AI HUB  |  SETUP GUIDE
Using Claude and GPT-4o in SAP Generative AI Hub: A Complete Setup Guide

Step-by-step instructions for deploying Claude Sonnet, Claude Haiku, and GPT-4o through SAP AI Core — with model selection guidance, working Python code, and a practical comparison for SAP-specific use cases. Updated following the official SAP + Anthropic partnership announced at Sapphire 2026.

By Deepa Dorairaj   |  SAP AI Solution Architect  |  Published May 2026

TL;DR: SAP and Anthropic announced an official partnership at Sapphire 2026 — Claude models are now the recommended path for HR and procurement agents in SAP's Autonomous Enterprise stack. This article covers how to deploy Claude Sonnet, Claude Haiku, and GPT-4o through SAP Generative AI Hub, when to use each model, and how to call them from Python using the sap-ai-sdk-gen SDK. No personal API keys required — everything runs through SAP's governed AI infrastructure.

Why This Article Now

SAP announced an official partnership with Anthropic at Sapphire 2026 — integrating Claude models to power agents in HR and procurement within SAP's Autonomous Enterprise stack. This formalizes what many practitioners have already been building: Claude through SAP's Generative AI Hub as a governed, compliant path to Anthropic's models without personal API keys or external infrastructure.

This article covers three models available through the Generative AI Hub today — Claude Sonnet 4, Claude Haiku 4.5, and GPT-4o — with complete deployment steps, working Python code, and a practical comparison of when to use each in SAP-specific workstreams.

PREREQUISITES 

What You Need Before Starting

  • SAP BTP account with SAP AI Core service instance provisioned
  • SAP AI Core service key (client ID, client secret, auth URL, AI API URL)
  • Generative AI Hub entitlement enabled on your AI Core instance
  • SAP AI Launchpad subscription (for UI-based deployment management)
  • Python 3.9+ environment (SAP BAS recommended for BTP-native development)

If you are new to SAP AI Core setup, complete Part 1 of the Generative AI Hub series first — it covers BTP provisioning, service key creation, and resource group setup from scratch. This article assumes AI Core is already running and accessible.

IMPORTANT: SDK PACKAGE NAME CHANGE 

Install the Correct SDK Package

The generative-ai-hub-sdk package is deprecated. The correct package is sap-ai-sdk-gen:

 

All import statements remain unchanged — only the installation command changes. This is a common setup error that causes ModuleNotFoundError at import time even after successful pip install.

 

# All imports remain the same after switching to sap-ai-sdk-gen
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.openai import ChatOpenAI
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.anthropic import ChatAnthropic
MODEL OVERVIEW 

The Three Models: What They Are and When to Use Them

 

Model

Model Name String

Executable

Best For

Claude Sonnet 4

anthropic--claude-sonnet-4

aws-bedrock

Long-context reasoning, structured document generation, HR and procurement agents, audit trail narratives, migration runbooks

Claude Haiku 4.5

anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5

aws-bedrock

High-volume classification, fast routing decisions, lightweight summarization, cost-sensitive workloads

GPT-4o

gpt-4o

azure-openai

ABAP code analysis, S/4HANA readiness assessment, SAP technical documentation, structured data extraction from SAP schemas

Claude Sonnet 4 is the recommended model for the SAP + Anthropic partnership use cases — HR agents and procurement agents. GPT-4o remains the stronger choice for ABAP-specific technical analysis. Claude Haiku 4.5 is the right choice when you need speed and volume at lower cost.

STEP 1: DEPLOY CLAUDE SONNET 4 

Creating a Claude Sonnet 4 Deployment

Via SAP AI Launchpad (UI)
  1. Open SAP AI Launchpad and select your workspace
  2. Navigate to ML Operations → Configurations → Create
  3. Set Configuration Name: Claude_Sonnet_4 (or your preferred name)
  4. Set Scenario: foundation-models
  5. Set Executable: aws-bedrock
  6. Set Model Name: anthropic--claude-sonnet-4
  7. Set Model Version: latest
  8. Save the configuration
  9. Navigate to ML Operations → Deployments → Create
  10. Select your Claude_Sonnet_4 configuration → Deploy
  11. Wait for status: RUNNING (typically 3-5 minutes)
  12. Copy the Deployment ID — you will need it for API calls
Via curl (API)
# Step 1: Get your bearer token
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AUTH_URL/oauth/token" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_ID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"
 
# Step 2: Create configuration
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/configurations" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"name":"Claude_Sonnet_4","executableId":"aws-bedrock",
       "scenarioId":"foundation-models",
       "parameterBindings":[{"key":"modelName","value":"anthropic--claude-sonnet-4"},
       {"key":"modelVersion","value":"latest"}]}"
 
# Step 3: Create deployment using the configurationId from Step 2
curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/deployments" ^
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^
  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^
  -d "{"configurationId":"YOUR_CONFIG_ID"}"
STEP 2: DEPLOY CLAUDE HAIKU 4.5 

Creating a Claude Haiku 4.5 Deployment

Follow the same steps as Claude Sonnet 4 with one change — set the model name to anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5:

# Configuration for Claude Haiku 4.5
  -d "{"name":"Claude_Haiku_45","executableId":"aws-bedrock",
       "scenarioId":"foundation-models",
       "parameterBindings":[{"key":"modelName","value":"anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5"},
       {"key":"modelVersion","value":"latest"}]}"

Claude Haiku 4.5 is significantly cheaper per token than Claude Sonnet 4. For workloads where you need to process high volumes of SAP records — classification, routing, summarization — Haiku is the cost-efficient choice. Reserve Sonnet 4 for tasks where output quality and reasoning depth matter most.

STEP 3: DEPLOY GPT-4O 

Creating a GPT-4o Deployment

GPT-4o uses the azure-openai executable rather than aws-bedrock:

  1. Create Configuration with Executable: azure-openai
  2. Set Model Name: gpt-4o
  3. Set Model Version: latest
  4. Deploy and wait for RUNNING status
# Configuration for GPT-4o
  -d "{"name":"GPT4o","executableId":"azure-openai",
       "scenarioId":"foundation-models",
       "parameterBindings":[{"key":"modelName","value":"gpt-4o"},
       {"key":"modelVersion","value":"latest"}]}"
STEP 4: CONFIGURE THE SDK 

Configuring sap-ai-sdk-gen

Create the AI Core configuration file at ~/.aicore/config.json:

{
  "AICORE_AUTH_URL": "https://YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com",
  "AICORE_CLIENT_ID": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
  "AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
  "AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP": "default",
  "AICORE_BASE_URL": "https://api.ai.prod.us-east-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com"
}

The AICORE_AUTH_URL must include the /oauth/token path when making direct API calls but NOT in the config.json file — the SDK appends it automatically. A common setup error is including /oauth/token in the config file, which causes authentication failures.

STEP 5: CALLING THE MODELS FROM PYTHON 

Working Python Code for All Three Models

Claude Sonnet 4 — Long-context reasoning and document generation
# Claude Sonnet 4 via SAP Generative AI Hub
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.anthropic import ChatAnthropic
 
llm_sonnet = ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-sonnet-4')
 
# Example: Generate a migration sign-off report
response = llm_sonnet.invoke('''
    Generate a post-migration sign-off report for the following:
    Objects migrated: CUSTOMER, VENDOR, MATERIAL
    Source count: 45,230 | Target count: 45,228
    Discrepancies: 2 records in CUSTOMER with missing tax classification
    Recommendation: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL
''')
print(response.content)
Claude Haiku 4.5 — High-volume classification and routing
# Claude Haiku 4.5 — fast and cost-efficient
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.anthropic import ChatAnthropic
 
llm_haiku = ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5')
 
# Example: Classify exception type for routing
response = llm_haiku.invoke('''
    Classify this SAP exception and return ONLY the exception type:
    FINANCIAL_VARIANCE | TECHNICAL_ERROR | DATA_QUALITY | SECURITY_VIOLATION
 
    Exception: Cost center 1000 has no valid assignment for
    purchase order 4500012345 dated 2026-05-01.
''')
print(response.content)  # Returns: FINANCIAL_VARIANCE
GPT-4o — ABAP code analysis and SAP technical tasks
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.openai import ChatOpenAI
 
llm_gpt4o = ChatOpenAI(proxy_model_name='gpt-4o')
 
# Example: S/4HANA readiness assessment on ABAP program
abap_code = '''
  SELECT * FROM mara INTO TABLE lt_mara.
  LOOP AT lt_mara INTO ls_mara.
    SELECT * FROM mseg WHERE matnr = ls_mara-matnr
    INTO TABLE lt_mseg.
  ENDLOOP.
'''
 
response = llm_gpt4o.invoke(f'''
    Analyze this ABAP code for S/4HANA readiness issues.
    Flag deprecated table accesses, performance anti-patterns,
    and clean core violations. Suggest specific fixes.
 
    Code: {abap_code}
''')
print(response.content)

Routing between models based on task type

# Model router — select the right model per task
def get_model_for_task(task_type: str):
    routing = {
        'abap_analysis':      ChatOpenAI(proxy_model_name='gpt-4o'),
        'document_generation': ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-sonnet-4'),
        'classification':     ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5'),
        'hr_agent':           ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-sonnet-4'),
        'procurement_agent':  ChatAnthropic(proxy_model_name='anthropic--claude-sonnet-4'),
    }
    return routing.get(task_type, ChatOpenAI(proxy_model_name='gpt-4o'))

# Usage
model = get_model_for_task('abap_analysis')
response = model.invoke('Analyze this ABAP program...')

The routing pattern above is the practical implementation of the model selection guidance from the comparison table. Build this as a shared utility in your agent codebase — any agent can call get_model_for_task() and get the right model without hardcoding model names throughout the application.

PRACTICAL MODEL COMPARISON FOR SAP USE CASES 

When to Use Which Model — SAP Practitioner Guide

 

Use Case

Recommended Model

Reason

Confidence

ABAP code analysis

GPT-4o

Strong ABAP training data, recognizes SAP-specific patterns

Best choice

S/4HANA readiness assessment

GPT-4o

Understands restricted APIs, clean core constraints

Best choice

Migration runbook generation

Claude Sonnet 4

Consistent structured long-form output, logical sequencing

Best choice

Post-migration sign-off report

Claude Sonnet 4

Stakeholder-ready formatting, nuanced risk narrative

Best choice

HR agent workflows

Claude Sonnet 4

Official SAP + Anthropic partnership recommendation

Recommended

Procurement agent workflows

Claude Sonnet 4

Official SAP + Anthropic partnership recommendation

Recommended

Exception classification

Claude Haiku 4.5

Fast, accurate, cost-efficient for high volume

Best choice

Readable, structured, traceable reasoning

SAP ABAP documentation

Best choice

Approval routing decisions

Best choice

COMMON SETUP ERRORS 

Troubleshooting Quick Reference

 

Error

Cause

Fix

ModuleNotFoundError: gen_ai_hub

Wrong package installed

pip install sap-ai-sdk-gen (not generative-ai-hub-sdk)

404 on model inference call

Wrong deployment ID or expired token

Verify deployment status RUNNING, generate fresh token

401 Unauthorized

Missing /oauth/token suffix or wrong credentials

Check AICORE_AUTH_URL in config — SDK appends /oauth/token automatically

Model not found error

Incorrect model name string

Use exact strings: anthropic--claude-sonnet-4, anthropic--claude-haiku-4-5, gpt-4o

Deployment stuck in Pending

Resource group not provisioned

Check resource group status in AI Launchpad — must be PROVISIONED

RBAC access denied

Service key missing admin scope

Recreate service key with aicore_admin role

entry.title: New SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the release of the SAP Activate Methodology Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2, now publicly available in the SAP Roadmap Viewer. This roadmap is designed to guide project teams, customers and partners alike, through a structured, end-to-end implementation of SAP Sales Cloud V2, from initial discovery all the way through to ongoing operations.

Whether you are embarking on a new implementation or looking to optimize an existing deployment, this roadmap provides a comprehensive framework of phases, deliverables, tasks, and accelerators tailored specifically to the SAP Sales Cloud V2 solution.

What Is This Roadmap?

The SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2 is a structured implementation guide built on the proven SAP Activate Methodology.

It organizes all project activities into sequential phases and workstreams, with accelerators (templates, guides, learning journeys, and help documentation) linked directly to the relevant tasks, making them easy to access when needed.

The roadmap covers:

  • SAP Sales Cloud V2 full implementation lifecycle
  • Integration with SAP ERP and SAP CPQ
  • Extensibility using SAP Build Apps and side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP
  • Ongoing operations, release management, and user adoption post go-live

Getting Started

To access the roadmap:

  1. Visit the SAP Activate road map Viewer  and search for “SAP Activate for SAP Sales Cloud V2”, or visit this link
  2. Log in to access additional content and accelerators (some accelerators require SAP Customer access)
  3. Use SAP Cloud ALM to manage your project tasks directly from the roadmap

Roadmap Structure: Phases at a Glance

The roadmap follows the six standard SAP Activate phases, each with activities and deliverables specific to SAP Sales Cloud V2.

Fig: Overview

🔍  Phase 1: Discover

Before the project formally begins, the Discover phase helps organizations evaluate the solution and plan their approach. Key activities include:

  • Try SAP Sales Cloud V2: Access a trial or demo environment to explore the solution
  • Benchmarking assessment: Evaluate current state and define success criteria
  • Data center selection: Choose the appropriate regional data center
  • Implementation strategy: Define the phasing approach and effort estimations
  • Scope statement: Develop the project scope and select the right cloud partner

🚀  Phase 2: Prepare

The Prepare phase establishes the foundation for a successful project. The focus is on project initiation, governance, and getting the team ready to execute.

  • Onboarding and system provisioning: Request the SAP Sales Cloud V2 tenant and set up SAP Cloud ALM
  • Customer team self-enablement: Team members self-enable on SAP Sales Cloud V2, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio, SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, and WalkMe
  • Project governance: Create the project charter, establish governance structures, define project standards, and conduct the kickoff workshop
  • Organizational Change Management (OCM): Develop the change concept, change plan, stakeholder analysis, and communication plan
  • Fit-to-Standard preparation: Prepare for the upcoming fit-to-standard workshops
  • Data migration planning: Develop the data migration approach and plan

💡  Key Accelerator:  The Onboarding Resource Center for SAP Sales Cloud V2 is your starting point for quickly getting the project team up to speed.  Onboarding Resource Center for SAP Sales Cloud V2

🔎  Phase 3: Explore

The Explore phase is where the solution is validated against business requirements through fit-to-standard analysis. Any gaps and configuration values identified are captured in the project backlog. Key activities include:

  • Fit-to-Standard Workshops: Conduct structured workshops covering organizational structure, master data, and sales transactional data — using CX-specific templates for fit-to-standard analysis
  • Backlog confirmation: Document business process flows, configuration values, and confirm the project backlog
  • Integration planning and design: Hand over integration requirements, determine customer-driven integrations, and review the integration list — including integration with SAP ERP and SAP CPQ
  • Extension planning and design: Detail the design for side-by-side extensibility for SAP Sales Cloud V2 on SAP BTP
  • Data load preparation: Determine required master and transactional data, download migration templates, and develop the overall load plan
  • Test planning: Create the test strategy, test plan, and define the test scope

💡  Key Accelerators:  Purpose-built CX templates for SAP Sales Cloud V2: CX Fit-2-Standard Master Data Template  |  CX Fit-2-Standard Transactional Data Template  |  CX Backlog Template

⚙️  Phase 4: Realize

The Realize phase is where the solution is built and tested through a series of agile sprints. Configuration, integration, extensions, data migration, and testing all happen here.

  • Sprint planning and execution: Iterative 2-week sprint cycles to incrementally configure and test the solution, starting with core settings and organizational structures, then progressing to detailed configuration
  • SAP Sales Cloud V2 – SAP CPQ integration: Set up the integration to enable seamless sales quote exchange between the two systems
  • SAP Sales Cloud V2 – SAP ERP integration: Configure integration for end-to-end business process continuity
  • Extension development: Set up and develop extensions in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2, including side-by-side extensions built on SAP BTP using SAP Build Apps
  • Data migration: Populate data into migration templates for SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and execute data migration in the test tenant
  • Test execution: Execute test cases, manage defects, and report on test results — supported by SAP Cloud ALM test management capabilities
  • Enablement content development and delivery: Create and deliver training materials to prepare end users for go-live
  • Production system request: Request the SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 production tenant and configure the production landscape

💡  Key Accelerators:  CX Test Management Guide  |  Implementing SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 (Learning Journey)  |  Integration of SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP CPQ

🚢  Phase 5: Deploy

The Deploy phase transitions the business to the new system. Key activities include:

  • End user learning: Conduct pilot learning sessions and deploy the full end-user training program
  • Production cutover: Verify non-transportable setup, execute data migration in the production system, and carry out all cutover tasks per the cutover plan
  • Post-production release tests: Validate the production system after go-live
  • Handover to support organization: Resolve and close open issues, and formally hand over operations responsibility

🔄  Phase 6: Run

The Run phase ensures the solution continues to deliver value after go-live. Key activities specific to SAP Sales Cloud V2 include:

  • Governance model: Establish ongoing governance and manage change requests
  • Incident and problem management: Manage incidents and problems through the support organization
  • Release and update cycles for SAP Sales Cloud V2: Manage updates and upgrades, and execute regression testing. The Release Navigator for Customer Experience consolidates essential release content organized by Lines of Business, helping teams stay current with new capabilities
  • Feature Activation Hub: Evaluate and activate new features delivered through SAP’s continuous delivery model — some features require explicit activation via the Feature Activation Hub app before they become available in the customer landscape
  • User adoption analysis: Measure objective user adoption KPIs post go-live, monitor user experience, and develop user adoption plans to drive sustained value

💡  Key Tool:  WalkMe — a digital adoption platform integrated into the SAP Sales Cloud V2 journey — helps organizations enhance user onboarding and enablement post go-live through interactive guides, walkthroughs, and insights into user behavior.

We look forward to supporting your SAP Sales Cloud V2 implementation journey. The roadmap will continue to evolve with new content, accelerators, and guidance as the product and methodology mature. Stay tuned for future updates!

For questions about SAP Activate, visit the SAP Activate Community page to find answers or post questions. You can also explore the SAP Activate Minute video playlist for quick, focused guidance on key methodology topics.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire for Data Pros: What Changes for You in the Next 12 Months
entry.content: The data product generation agent was my favorite announcement from SAP Sapphire because it finally delivers on the  data products creation experience we have been talking about. It gives data engineers and modelers an AI copilot that can walk them step‑by‑step through the process of designing, building, and publishing a data product, while still keeping them in control of every decision. It allows you to build a data product by starting with the business need - and then letting the agent propose schemas, transformations, and joins - and then you can refine with your domain knowledge. The combination of accelerating delivery, embedding best practices while letting the experts steer is exactly the type of use case that will provide real ROI to an organization from the bottom up.

entry.title: Re: Considerations when implementing IAS for SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content: Regarding your second point, we have occasionally had this occur.  Several areas we've identified as potential culprits include changing the start date in onboarding and not changing the target start date in the offer letter (and the changed offer not having approval); changes to the position after the record is in onboarding, including changes to the supervisor.  In order to process a start date change, we have to cancel the onboarding record, confirm the target start date in the offer letter is the new offer start date and that the offer letter is approved, confirm the position record associated with the requisition is correct and then reinitiate the onboarding.

entry.title: Re: Restrict, form output specific to plant in s4hana clo...

Hello!

In Public Cloud the clean way is to control it in app Output Parameter Determination.

First select Show Rules for Quality Notification -> select Determination Step = Output Type

 Next maintain the business rule in Decision Table.

Notification tyepe =  Q2

Plant =  the plants you need this output only

Select your Output Type (your letter)

With this settings only these plants will ever generate the complaint letter. Other plants behave like the form doesn’t exist.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/4a82a52b463043f5a80c14b0ec82fc34.html?locale=en-US&q=Output+Parameter+Determination

entry.title: Re: Output Parameter Determination Invoice Send To Custom...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Generated Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the procedure works for you:

To address the issue of the missing E-invoice QR code and E-invoice number on the smart form, you should consider the following steps:

  1. Check Output Parameter Determination Configuration:  Ensure that the output parameter determination is correctly configured to include the E-invoice details in the output. This involves setting the correct form template and ensuring that the output type is configured to include the necessary E-invoice information.
    For more information, see Output Parameter Determination and How to Configure Output Parameter Determination fo… - SAP Community

  2. Verify Custom Logic Implementation: Since you have implemented custom logic using the BAdI APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS, ensure that this logic correctly handles the inclusion of E-invoice details. The logic should ensure that the E-invoice number and QR code are fetched and passed to the form template.

  3. Form Template Customization:  Check if the form template used for the smart form is designed to include the E-invoice QR code and number. You may need to customize the form template using Adobe LiveCycle Designer to ensure these fields are included and correctly mapped.
    For more information, see 2924014 - Address formatting on billing documents output - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  4. Integration with E-document Cockpit: Ensure that the integration with the E-document cockpit is correctly set up to pass the E-invoice details to the output management system. This includes verifying that the E-invoice data is correctly generated and available in the system

  5. Testing and Debugging: Conduct thorough testing to ensure that the E-invoice details are correctly included in the output. Use debugging tools to trace the flow of data and identify any points where the E-invoice information might be missing or incorrectly processed.

By following these steps, you should be able to identify and resolve the issue with the missing E-invoice QR code and number on the smart form.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Auto Fill Serial Numbers in Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
#GenAI-Generated Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I've asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the procedure works for you:

To automatically fill serial numbers in an Outbound Delivery through a custom program in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you need to ensure that the serial number profile is correctly set up in the material master record. The serial number profile should include procedures that enable the assignment of serial numbers for deliveries. For example, the procedure ‘SDLS’ allows the creation of serial numbers on delivery,  and the usage should be set to ‘02 = can’. This setup ensures that serial numbers can be assigned during the delivery process.

For more information, see Serial Number Profile and Serial Numbers in Sales and Delivery Management.

If you are facing issues with filling the serial number, it might be due to the serial number profile not being correctly configured or the necessary procedures not being included. Ensure that the serial number profile is properly maintained and that the procedures like ‘SDLS’ are active for the materials involved in the outbound delivery.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Need logic for Custom report in SAP PM/EAM Equipment Performance Report
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Could you please provide logic to fetch this Field in SAP PM/EAM for Custom report, 
If you could specifically help me with the Logic for 
" Actual Progress Field " 

Below is the logic I have used
Total Activity required I'm providing in the Input screen. 
Also the No. of Days Required = Total Activity Required ÷ (Equipment Capacity × Running Hours)
Expected Activity Progress = Equipment Capacity × Running HRS
For Running Hours I used the logic 
Pass ATNAM = RUNNING_HOURS to CABN and fetch ATINN value.
Pass MPOBJ = IE*Equipment Number* (EQUNR) and ATINN to table IMPTT to fetch the corresponding POINT.
Pass the derived POINT to table IMRG and fetch records within the selected date range (IDATE).
Filter records for each day and consider CDIFF (difference value) for the measuring point.
Group the records by IDATE (Activity Date) and calculate the SUM of CDIFF for each date.

Thanks!

Plant/Project Plant DescriptionEquipment NoEquipment DescriptionEquipment CapacityCapacity UnitRunning HRSActivity DateTotal activity requiredNo of days required to complete ActivityEstimated ProgressActual Progress

entry.title: Re: I want to restrict the edit functionality after the i...
entry.content:
Hello!

The requirement cannot be solved through role configuration alone. The solution requires two layers working together: a clean role design and a custom logic rule that enforces the restriction at the application level based on document status.

 

Business Role Redesign:

The goal here is to ensure the invoice initiator's role carries only the access their job function genuinely requires.

Identify the relevant business catalogs -> open the sap Fiori apps reference library and look up the apps your initiators use.

In the Maintain Business Roles app, create a copy of the role currently assigned to invoice initiators. Review and tighten Write access.

This step alone will not block editing of posted documents, because write access is not document-status-aware.

Lastly assign the new role to initiator users -> replace the existing role assignment with the new custom role for all invoice initiators.

 

Custom Logic

This is where the actual restriction is enforced. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard extensibility framework called Custom Logic (search for app Custom Logic).

Search within Custom Logic for BAdIs related to supplier invoice processing, specifically ones that fire on a save or change event.Here implement the restriction rule.

The logic to be implemented does three things in sequence:

- Check whether the invoice document already has a posted status, meaning an FI accounting document has been generated against it.

- Check whether the user currently attempting the change is the original creator of the document.

- If both conditions are true, raise an error message that prevents the save and clearly tells the user to use a credit memo or reversal instead.

If the requirement is to block editing for all users, not just the initiator, the logic can be simplified to check only the document status, regardless of who the current user is.

 

If the specific BAdI needed for this restriction may not be available in the Custom Logic app. If that happens, the recommended path is to raise an SAP support and ask SAP to confirm the supported extensibility point for enforcing edit restrictions on posted supplier invoices in the Public Cloud edition.

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
Hi @DEEPA_DORAIRAJ 

File Input issue in orchestration API

We need to pass file-input in the orchestration API. But the SDK suggests the prompt to be passed as messages in chat completion. Due to this we had to hardcode the prompt in the code . Providing the prompt in the template is not working and giving error. Is there any way we can create a template for the prompt and pass that in the file input call. 
Provided the  example below of the usage. Looking for a way we can maintain the user or at least the system prompt as prompt template. Right now, neither of them works in prompt template.

 const client = new OrchestrationClient(
          {
            promptTemplating: {
              model: { name: 'anthropic--claude-4.5-sonnet', params: { n: 1, temperature: 0 } },
              prompt: {
                template: [
                  {
                    role: 'system',
                    content:
                      'You are a document text extraction expert. Extract the complete text from the attached PDF file(s) exactly as it appears.\n\n' +
                      'Return ONLY a JSON array — no explanation, no markdown.\n\n' +
                      'The user message lists the fileId for each file. Use those exact values.\n\n' +
                      'Format: [{ "fileId": "<id>", "content": "<full extracted text>" }]',
                  },
                ],
              },
            },
          },
          { resourceGroup: AI_TEMPLATES.RESOURCE_GROUP },
          { destinationName: AI_TEMPLATES.DESTINATION }
        );

        // Build an explicit ordered ID list so the LLM knows exactly which fileId
        // to return for each attached PDF. The filename property on the file block is a
        
        const fileIdList = lcFiles
          .map((file, i) => `File ${i + 1}: fileId="${String(file.ID)}"`)
          .join(', ');

        const response = await client.chatCompletion({
          messages: [
            {
              role: 'user', // file type only supported in 'user' role
              content: [
                {
                  type: 'text',
                  text: `Files (use these exact fileId values in your JSON response): ${fileIdList}`, // content is in the file blocks below
                },
                ...fileContentBlocks,
              ],
            },
          ],
        });

SAP AI Core

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core: Deployment and Configuration Errors and How to Fix Them (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
hi @KM11 

Good question and a known limitation worth documenting clearly.

The root cause is that the Orchestration SDK's prompt template system currently doesn't support file-type content blocks in the template definition itself — templates are designed for text-based message structures. File inputs using the filecontent type must be passed at runtime in the chatCompletion() call, specifically in the user role messages, which is exactly what your implementation is doing.

For the system prompt specifically — your current approach of hardcoding it in the promptTemplating.prompt.template array is actually the correct pattern. The system prompt doesn't need to be dynamic for file extraction use cases since it's instructional rather than data-driven. What you're hitting is that you can't combine a template-defined system prompt with a runtime file input in the same template reference — the SDK treats them as separate concerns.

The cleanest workaround for maintaining the system prompt as a configurable template while still passing file inputs at runtime:

Define the system prompt in a separate configuration constant or environment variable rather than a prompt template stored in AI Core. At runtime, construct the full message array combining your configured system prompt with the dynamic user message and file content blocks — exactly as your current code does. This gives you the maintainability of a template without fighting the SDK's file input constraints.

const SYSTEM_PROMPT = process.env.EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT || 
  'You are a document text extraction expert...';

const response = await client.chatCompletion({
  messages: [
    { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
    { role: 'user', content: [
        { type: 'text', text: `Files: ${fileIdList}` },
        ...fileContentBlocks
    ]}
  ]
});

This is a limitation worth raising with SAP support under component CA-ML-AIP — file input support in prompt templates is a reasonable feature request and documenting it helps the product team prioritize it.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hi,

For the recommendation on when to use a new position, you may refer to the following IDP, chapter 6.7.5, Updating Existing Position Vs. Using a New/ Vacant Position:

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Position Management: Design Considerations and Recommendations (https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/implementation-design-principles)

Technically, both scenarios are possible (publish from Compensation and reuse of the same position), with synchronization from Job Information to Position. For this topic, please refer to the Position Transfer and Position Reclassification

Depending on the customer process, reusing the position, even when there is a change in Job Classification and/or Pay Grade may not be an issue. If, for instance, Pay Grade is relevant for headcount planning, it may be a problem, because the "implicit cost" of the position will be different.

Thanks,

Takashi

entry.title: Warehouse and Transportation Synchronization in SAP S/4HANA Using SAP EWM and TM
entry.content:
Abstract & Introduction

During SAP TM and SAP EWM integration projects, many consultants are able to configure basic transportation planning and warehouse execution processes individually, but struggle when integrating both systems together in real-time business scenarios. as per my previous implementations, I observed that transportation planning worked correctly in SAP TM, while Transportation Units, loading instructions, freight settlement integration, or warehouse execution synchronization failed because of small missing integration configurations.

Biggest challenges in SAP TM and EWM integration is like the process spans multiple components including SD, MM, TM, EWM, and FI. Although SAP standard documentation explains individual configuration nodes, it does not clearly explain the sequence of integration dependencies, organizational assignments, Freight Unit formation logic, or the practical impact of missing configurations during end-to-end execution and settlement testing.

This article was written from a practitioner perspective to help my fellow consultants understand not only the configuration activities, but also the operational flow and common integration dependencies involved in real project environments.  

In this document, simulating a scenario starting from Sales to delivery of the goods to final customer and settlement of freight cost. Herein SAP MM, SD, EWM, TM & FI should be well integrated however focus is majorly on SAP EWM and TM.

(SD- Sales and distribution, MM-material management, TM-Transport management, EWM-Extended warehouse management, FI-Finance)

Fig1: SAP SD + SAP TM – Operational process steps

Fig2: SAP TM + SAP EWM – Operational process steps

Fig3: SAP EWM + SAP TM – Operational process steps

Fig4: SAP TM + SAP MM - Operational process steps

Customization

#1 Assignment of location (1003) to EWM target system/Warehouse number (/SCMTMS/LOC3):

It is used to manage transportation location (shipping point) that is required in transportation planning and logistics execution. The assignment of location 1003 to the EWM target system and warehouse number in /SCMTMS/LOC3 ensures that SAP TM can correctly identify the EWM warehouse responsible for transportation execution activities such as loading, unloading, and warehouse processing.

Sometimes, I have seen people use other location 1001 or 1002 but they miss 1003, in that they struggle later while unit testing. 

#2 Define Integration profile:

It is used to control, how TM documents and EWM documents communicate with each other. It clearly says that how loading/unloading instructions to EWM. This also includes Transportation unit in loading/unloading instructions.

SPRO -> TM-> Integration -> Define EWM Integration Profile

If this configuration is not done well, the loading instruction will not go to EWM and transportation unit will not be populated in SAP EWM with assigned deliveries, so it will take lot to struggle for consultant to identify the root cause.

#3 Link Freight Order (REFO) with EWM integration profile:

SPRO->TM->Freight order management->Freight Order->Define freight order type

This configuration is used to create and control different types of Freight Orders and make a link between freight order and EWM integration profile. With this integration only, loading/unloading info go to EWM for that particular Fright order types.

#4 Link between packaging material and Means of transport: /SCWM/PM_MTR:

This defines which packaging materials are allowed or suitable for specific means of transport in SAP EWM and TM integration. This is particularly used for transportation planning like what type of transport it would be like Truck, Airplane, container etc.

#5 Activate PPF message: SPPFC (choose /SCWM/SHP_REV and /SCMWM/TU): 

This is the backbone of SAP EWM and TM integration, wherein action definitions need to be activated for major action profile. Action Definition Activation means enabling an automatic SAP system action so it can execute during a business process based on defined conditions.

Example: 

Final Action = Send Freight Order to carrier
when this will Trigger = On Save
When Condition is fulfilled = Carrier assigned
if only Activation = YES, then only it will be automatically

This configuration should NOT be missed.

#6 Map Purchase organization to ERP account assignment: 

This is also one of the critical configurations, which links SAP TM purchasing organizations with ERP accounting structures so freight procurement and settlement costs can post correctly into ERP finance. 

If you miss or wrongly assign the cost center/Internal order then Service PO creation will be failed and accounting postings may go wrong, and finally this will impact the settlement. T

SPRO->Transport Management->Settlement->Forwarding Settlement->Setting for posting Intracompany settlements->Assign Internal order/Cost center to sender organization

#7 Organization unit:

Need to configure the organization elements specially purchase organization and purchasing groups. This tell, which department/company/team is managing this whole transportation process. Like who owns Transportation Planning or Purchasing & Carrier Procurement etc.

SPRO->Transport Management->Master data->Organizational Management->Organizational model->Create organizational model

Created org unit for my purchase organization REPO and purchasing group called RPG. One more important is the sequence, first BP and then create organization unit by assigning BP and configuration entity(Purchase org/Group).

#8 Control parameters:

This parameter control, which document will be sent(integrate) to EWM, whether Sales order or delivery.

In this document, however I am sending Delivery document to TM for Integration, as selected Outbound in below configuration.

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define document for document integration

#9 Logistics Integration profile:

This tells SAP TM how transportation requirements should be converted into Freight Units for planning and execution. This is a part of Freight unit formation and also includes the freight unit building rule. Sequence is Freight unit building rule (FUBR) and then logistics integration profile by assigning the FUBR. Moreover, FUBR dictate how deliveries will be grouped into fright unit using same delivery route, same delivery date etc.

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define logistics integration profile for SD,MM,LE

#10 Determine Control key and Logistics Integration profile:

SPRO->Transport Management->Integration->Logistics Integration->Internal TM component Integration->Define transportation relevance of delivery documents

This configuration node fetches the control key and Logistics integration profile for the combination of shipping point and Delivery type.

Master data

#1 Business Partner(BP):

Vendor as a Plant:

Created Vendor/BP 47/600135 for plant and assigned to plant.

Assigned BP/Vendor to vendor

Carrier:

A carrier is the transportation company that moves goods, and SAP stores it as a Business Partner master record. Below RE_CA_TM carrier was created using Tcode BP with prescribed roles, especially Carrier profile "CRM010"

#2 Resource Master:

Resource Master in SAP TM represents the physical transportation equipment or vehicle used to execute transportation activities and shipment planning.

Example: one company has 100 trucks then each truck is created as a Resource Master. Created truck RE_10_TK_LTL as a resource.

End to end Transactions

#1 Sales order(VA01): 

Created Sales order # #1960 for material RE-FUEL-TANK for customer(RE_CU_TM).

 

#2 Outbound Delivery(VL01N):

Crated outbound delivery for above sales order and a new tab "TM Status. This signifies SAP S4HANA delivery is well integrated with SAP TM. Moreover, this Tab is coming, during Delivery creation (not Sales order creation), because integration made at delivery level.  Just for information please check above mentioned node "Define document for document integration"

 

If you select the "TM Status", then you can see Freight unit which is first stage of Transport Management.

#3 Freight Unit:

A Freight Unit is A transportation demand, which clears what to transport, from where, to where, quantity and finally delivery dates.

Under this step, we are opening the Freight unit (using web based TM system), which is associated with particular delivery.

Goto Planning tab->Select the Freight unit Worklist

#4 Freight order:

A Freight Order is actual transportation execution document, which is used for plan the truck, assigning carrier, route execution and shipment tracking. so a freight order contains Carrier, Vehicle, Source Location, Destination, Pickup Date, Freight Units etc.

Under this step, creating Freight order by using below highlighted path.

Assigning Truck resource and Carrier 

#5 Displaying Freight order:

Freight order contains Carrier, Vehicle, Source Location, Destination, Pickup Date, Freight Units etc. Below Freight order screen shot shows the document flow, includes Freight unit, Outbound delivery and sales order. It is also showing freight amount, as applicable.

#6 Consignment order:

consignment order is a business document used to group multiple transportation requirements (freight units) based on shared criteria such as source/destination location, delivery date/time, and business partner. It acts as a communication tool between the supplier (consignor) and the customer (consignee), indicating which products will be delivered together and when.

Below, manually creating the consignment order through Freight order

#7 Send loading Instructions to EWM

Under this step, sending loading instructions to EWM, which will help to send the Freight order to be distributed to Transportation unit in SAP-TM. 

#8 Send Instructions:

This step is used to process the PPF action but can also schedule a background job for processing the PPF actions. To make this happen immediately, executed this step.

#9 Check Transportation unit:

This used to check whether Transportation unit is created (Same as Freight order). If it is not populating that means, PPF actions in previous step not executed successfully.

#10 Creation and confirmation of Warehouse task:

Now Transportation unit is created with assigned delivery, and this step will do warehouse task creation process. This step will be done in EWM using the tcode /SCWM/TU

#11 Warehouse task creation(Picking process): 

This step is the part of picking process, here warehouse task is being created against outbound delivery order. Here warehouse task is creating manually under tcode /SCWM/PRDO

Warehouse order #6 created.

#12 Confirming Warehouse task/Order

As after creation the warehouse task/order, immediate step is to confirm the warehouse task. which can be done using scanner(RFUI) but here confirming the same, using the manual option.

#13 Loading + Goods Issue completion (EWM):

Once material is picked and next step is loading the material in truck and complete the goods issues process. In this step, completing loading and goods issue at Transportation unit level (now this information will go to SAP TM, and goods issue information will go to S4HANA for Inventory updates).

#14 Freight settlement document

In SAP Transportation Management (TM), a freight settlement document (FSD) is essential for managing transportation costs and facilitating invoice verification with suppliers or carriers. other words Freight Settlement Document is the document used to calculate and settle transportation costs with the carrier. this contains Freight charges, Taxes, Carrier details, Shipment reference and Cost calculation.

#15 Settlement:

Settlement Process in SAP TM is the process of calculating transportation charges, creating freight settlement documents, and sending the costs to finance for carrier payment. Transaction wise, it will create service purchase order and service entry sheet automatically (if SAP TM and SAP MM+ SAP FI are integrated well).

In case of error, it will not create service PO or service entry sheet and finally FSD will not be invoiced.

Conclusion

In real SAP projects, many integration issues do not originate from core transportation planning itself, but from missing integration dependencies between TM, EWM, MM, and FI configurations. May be a small configuration gaps such as incomplete PPF activation, incorrect purchasing organization mapping, missing warehouse-location assignments, or improperly maintained organizational elements can significantly impact Transportation Unit creation, warehouse execution, freight settlement, and settlement postings. Similarly, approaches may vary depending on embedded or decentralized EWM landscapes, customer-specific transportation processes, warehouse automation requirements, and freight settlement strategies.

Apart from normal configuration steps, my fellow consultants should focus on understanding the integration dependencies, business process sequencing, communication flow between systems, and operational impact of each configuration object. 

 

Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMCORP etc. He worked on various type of project flavors like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

entry.title: Debug command "Suspend" in Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
I'm wondering what the debug command "Suspend" is for, when it can be selected, when to use it. Each time I look at the button, it's inactive.

NB:

  • The command "Suspend" didn't exist in the "old/new" SAP GUI backend debugger.
  • I couldn't find any explanation in the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/navigating-in-debugger?utm_source=chatgpt.com&locale=en-US 

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Debug command "Suspend" in Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
The command "Suspend" can be selected in the Eclipse ADT debugger when the debugger is "stepping", i.e. when the debugger has been started (i.e. it has stopped on a first break point) and the code is currently running (i.e. when continuing after a break point and before the next break point, if any).

The command "Suspend" suspends the execution as soon as possible (technical limitations like SQL running, minimal amount of time), anywhere in the code.

One reason to use it is when the code runs for a long time without pausing at one of your breakpoints, and you suspect a performance issue or an endless loop.

One frequent case is that a background job is running for a long time and has to be debugged for analyzing:

  • Start SM51 via Eclipse ADT, select the workprocess and then the menu Process > Program > Debug
  • The debugger starts anywhere in the code
  • You can then check what happens, add breakpoints to better analyze
  • If you continue the job and your breakpoints are still not reached, you may run the command "Suspend" to suspend the execution and start debugging.

Remarks about the "old/new" SAP GUI backend debugger:

  • The command equivalent to "Suspend" was quite exhausting: SM51 > Select the workprocess > menu Process > Program > Debug, and often did not work the second and next times (no idea why, maybe one workaround was to continue the execution by stopping the backend debugger via the menu Debugger > Exit (Application continues running)).
  • "Suspend" was missing because, if I'm not wrong, it was technically impossible because only one process could be active at a moment, either the debugged process or the debuggee process, i.e. when the debugged process was running it was not possible to click a debug option in the debuggee process as its SAP GUI window was inactive.

entry.title: Re: BADI for Mass Schedule Maintenance Plans
entry.content:
Email Notification for Preventive Maintenance Orders (S/4HANA Cloud 2602)

There are two approaches to send email notifications when preventive maintenance orders are automatically generated via IP10/IP30:

Option 1: Flexible Workflow for Maintenance Orders (Recommended)

Use Fiori app F4989 – Flexible Workflow for Maintenance Orders.

Configuration approach:

  • Define global preconditions such as:

    • Order Type (preventive)
    • Maintenance Plan is not empty (ensures trigger from IP10/IP30)
    • Planning Plant / Planner Group (optional)
  • Add a Notification Only step:
    • Assign recipients (e.g., partner on order, planner group, person responsible, or specific users)
    • Set execution to auto complete in My Inbox
  • Email setup:
    • Use Fiori app Manage Email Templates
    • Copy standard template:  SWT_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS
    • Follow naming convention: YY_02000019_CRT_10
    • Add Subject: Alert: Maintenance Order {{OrderNumber}} from PM is generated
    • Add Body: Maintenance Order {{OrderNumber}} has been generated via Maintenance Plan for Technical Object {{TechnicalObject}}. Please review your technical log.
  • Prerequisites:
    • User email maintained in Maintain Business User / Manage Business Partner
    • Preventive order type assigned to Phase Model Profile (workflow trigger based on status change to Approval)

Option 2: BAdI-Based Enhancement (Advanced / Extension Approach)

For more ABAP logic, use BADI_EAM_MO_CH_BEF_SAVE with ABAP Cloud extensibility.

Approach:

  • Trigger logic in BAdI when:

    • Order Type (preventive)
    • Maintenance Plan is not empty (ensures trigger from IP10/IP30)
    • Planning Plant / Planner Group (optional)

  • Send email using: CL_BCS_MAIL_MESSAGE

  • Key steps:

    • Key steps: Set sender using .set_sender()
    • Add recipients dynamically via .add_recipient() (e.g., from I_MaintenanceOrder CDS view)
    • Define subject using .set_subject()
    • Send asynchronously using .send_asynchronous() (to avoid transactional impact)

Recommendation

  • Use Flexible Workflow (Option 1) for a standard, clean core, and configuration-driven solution
  • Use BAdI (Option 2) only if additional logic or flexibility is required beyond workflow capabilities

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Issue of adding serial numbers to sales return delive...
entry.content:
The customer returns delivery API listed here - https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_CUSTOMER_RETURN_DELIVERY_SRV_0002/path/post_A_ReturnsDeliveryHeader includes the capability for inclusion of serial numbers under the maintenance object list portion of the schema.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby
